goal: Assess whether the v1.1 multi-agent chain actually enforces validation/approval/budget/fallback at the main-agent layer and whether writeback and role-context/budget fuse are implemented as real runtime checks rather than documentation only.
summary: The v1.1 multi-agent chain in codex-global-multi-agent is not only documented but backed by a concrete validator script that checks subagent payloads, enforces memory_access_mode, validates evidence and state_delta shape, calculates a per-stage and long-term context budget, and derives an approval_report plus contract_assertions; however, the actual writeback into OpenMemory and the persistence of audit records are still described as main-agent responsibilities in memory_write_policy.md rather than implemented in code in this repo, so writeback remains a documented contract rather than a fully wired runtime path.

evidence.value: subagent_contract schema and README protocol description
existing v11 run artifacts and planner/verifier reports

facts.value: scripts/validate_and_merge.py implements a real validation and merging step that inspects top-level JSON fields, state_delta facts/preferences/decisions/risks, evidence typing, fallback_suggestion values, and memory_access_mode for all subagent outputs in the v1.1 Research Pipeline.

The validator computes a budget_report using max-stage and max-long-term token parameters, aggregating per-file stage_context_tokens and long_term_tokens to determine whether the total context usage stays within configured limits for the stage.

The validator builds an approval_report over the merged state_delta, classifying each proposed fact/preference/decision/risk into approved or deferred based on scope, source, and presence of evidence_ids.

Contract assertions include checks for unique long-term memory entry (memory_access_mode == main_agent_packaged_context_only for all role-bearing payloads), structured proposals presence, approval_writeback_ready flag, budget_fuse_ok, and fallback_routing_ok, and they are combined into a final report.ok boolean that can gate writeback.

memory_write_policy.md states that the main agent exclusively owns long-term memory queries and writes and that subagents may only submit candidate state_delta items for review, not perform direct writes.

subagent_contract.schema.json enforces the presence of goal, scope, allowed_tools, summary, evidence, state_delta, risks, fallback_suggestion, next_steps, and confidence, and constrains memory_access_mode to main_agent_packaged_context_only for this pipeline.

decisions.summary: Treat scripts/validate_and_merge.py as the canonical v1.1 approval gate for subagent outputs.
decisions.details: Given the presence of structured validation, budget computation, and approval_report/contract_assertions, the main agent should call validate_and_merge.py as the approve stage for research_pipeline_v11 runs before attempting any writeback to long-term memory.

decisions.summary: Keep long-term writeback in the main agent rather than in this repo.
decisions.details: memory_write_policy.md explicitly assigns writeback and audit_record generation to the main agent; this repo only builds approval_report and contract_assertions, so the main agent should consume those artifacts and perform writes into OpenMemory externally.

risks.summary: Approval pipeline exists but writeback into OpenMemory is not wired in this repo.
risks.details: While validate_and_merge.py derives approval_report and contract_assertions, there is no implementation here that takes approved entries and writes them into OpenMemory or logs audit records; this remains a responsibility of the main agent runtime.

risks.summary: Budget fuse is enforced at the validation stage only.
risks.details: The budget_report and budget_fuse_ok assertion operate at the report/approval stage; the main agent must still honor these limits when constructing actual prompts or long-term memory injections, otherwise runtime context usage could drift beyond what was validated.

risks: Writeback into OpenMemory and audit_record generation are still only specified in memory_write_policy.md and not implemented as concrete code paths in this repo, so there is a gap between approval_report and actual persistence that the main agent must bridge correctly.

Budget enforcement currently lives in validate_and_merge.py as a reporting and gating mechanism; if the main agent ignores budget_report or contract_assertions.budget_fuse_ok, context pollution risks and over-injection remain.
Because this repo does not contain the main agent loop, it cannot be fully verified here whether approval_report is always used before any long-term memory writeback in live runs.
next_steps: Have the main agent implementation explicitly consume validation_report_v11.json and map approval_report.approved entries into OpenMemory writeback calls and audit_record objects, in line with memory_write_policy.md.
Add a lightweight checklist or script in this repo that demonstrates how the main agent should interpret contract_assertions.budget_fuse_ok and abort or shrink injections when it is false.

Optionally capture a sample validation_report_v11.json from a full research_pipeline_v11 run and review it manually to ensure approved decisions match expectations before enabling automatic writeback.
Document in the main enhancement guide that long-term memory writes must go through validate_and_merge.py and must not bypass the approval_report/contract_assertions gate.
